title: abies lasiocarpa subsp. arizonica
intro: Abies lasiocarpa subsp. arizonica, also known as Arizona fir, is a subspecies of fir tree native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Arizona fir include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Arizona fir typically include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other topical applications.
